div.head: 236. Editorial Note
div.p: On December 7, 1961, the Department of State, reacting to the Japanese Government’s protest of the President’s November 21 request for a Tariff Commission investigation on the establishment of an equalization fee on cotton textiles from Japan, sent telegram 1465 to the Embassy in Tokyo. This telegram, serving as guidance, outlined several points with which to respond to the protest, and is scheduled for publication in volume XXII.
